---
title: "MDS6"
author: "Trish"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

`
```{r calling libraries}
library(tidyverse)
```


```{r reading in data}
#read in mds scores for each sample forming a dataframe with score and file name

mds_files <- list.files("C:/Users/pquin/OneDrive - National University of Ireland, Galway/Research Project/FinaleDB/mdsScores/bp6_mdsScores", pattern = "mds6$", full.names = TRUE)

mds.df <- tibble(file = basename(mds_files), 
mds_score = map_dbl(mds_files, ~ {
  as.numeric(readLines(.x))
  
})
)

#want to add an extra column to df to call treatment as cancer/control
mds.df <- mds.df %>%
  mutate(treatment = if_else(
    str_detect(file, "^Ascites"),
    "cancer",
    "control"
  ))

```
```{r graphing mds}

ggplot(mds.df) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(x = treatment, y = mds_score, fill = treatment)) +
  labs(title = "MDS scores for 6bp End−Motifs",
       y = "MDS") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Treatment",
    values = c(
      "cancer" = "pink", 
      "control" = "lightblue"
    ))+
  theme_bw()

ggsave("MDS6bpEM.png", width = 7, height = 5)


```
